name: Phase Close — CHANGELOG, Green Validator, Release-Decision Human Gate

files_modify.purpose: Replace the `_No unreleased changes._` placeholder under the existing `## [Unreleased]` heading with real `### Added` / `### Changed` entries covering all four phase-002 deliverables: (1) ADR-001 (`docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md`) recording the CI-compliance-enforcement decision, plus the matching `product_spec.md` §2.1 layout amendment bumping `spec_version` 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0 (feature 011); (2) `tools/validate.sh`, the repo-local NDS compliance validator (C1-C8), its `tools/fixtures/` negative fixtures, and its `NIZAM.json` capability-index entry (feature 012); (3) `.github/workflows/compliance.yml`, the CI gate running the validator on `pull_request` and `push` to `main` (feature 013); (4) the four methodology 0.2.0 hardening sections — the External Anchor Rule and Mandatory Adversarial Evidence requirement in `methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md`, the Evidence Capture Convention and Clock-Read Timestamps rule in `methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md`, the Plan Amendment Rule in `methodology/00_planning.md`, and the Release Mechanics Ownership section in `methodology/05_release_train.md` (feature 014). `## [Unreleased]` MUST remain the first version heading in the file, positioned above `## [0.1.0] - 2026-07-08` exactly as today (Keep a Changelog convention — the ordering lesson from contract 007). The `[0.1.0]` section and everything below it is untouched byte-for-byte. Per `methodology/05_release_train.md` §6 (Release Mechanics Ownership, added in feature 014), the eventual v0.2.0 roll-up of these `[Unreleased]` entries into a dated `## [0.2.0]` heading, and the git tag itself, are release mechanics owned by the release-manager/orchestrator role AFTER human sign-off — this contract does NOT perform that roll-up and does NOT create any tag.

non_goals: No creation of a v0.2.0 git tag — the release decision is recorded as a human gate only; the pipeline never cuts the tag

No version-section roll-up — CHANGELOG.md's [Unreleased] entries are NOT folded into a new dated [0.2.0] heading in this contract; that roll-up is release mechanics per methodology/05_release_train.md §6, performed by the release-manager/orchestrator role only after human sign-off

No edits to docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md, tools/validate.sh, tools/fixtures/*, .github/workflows/compliance.yml, NIZAM.json, or any methodology/*.md file — those are the already-complete deliverables of features 011-014 being described, not re-implemented, here
No edit to any schema/*.json or standard/*.md file
No edit to the existing [0.1.0] section of CHANGELOG.md or anything below it
No edit to .agent/run_state.json, .agent/feature_list_002.json, or any completed .agent/contracts/*.json, .agent/qa/*.json, or .agent/evidence/* file — those are immutable audit records
No edit to any file beyond CHANGELOG.md and this contract file

verification.acceptance_test: CHANGELOG.md has an [Unreleased] section naming the phase-002 deliverables: grep -q '## \[Unreleased\]' CHANGELOG.md && grep -Eqi 'validate\.sh|compliance|CI enforcement' CHANGELOG.md -- STRENGTHENED (Keep-a-Changelog ordering lesson from contract 007): also asserts, by line number, that '## [Unreleased]' is the FIRST version heading in the file and remains positioned above '## [0.1.0]', and that the deliverable phrasing is found specifically within the Unreleased section's own body (not merely somewhere in the whole file), so a check anchored on the wrong section cannot vacuously pass.
verification.command: f=CHANGELOG.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; first_heading_line=$(grep -n '^## \[' "$f" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); u_line=$(grep -n '^## \[Unreleased\]' "$f" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); test -n "$u_line" || { echo "no '## [Unreleased]' heading found"; exit 1; }; test "$first_heading_line" = "$u_line" || { echo "[Unreleased] (line $u_line) is not the first version heading (first is line $first_heading_line) -- ordering violation"; exit 1; }; v_line=$(grep -n '^## \[0\.1\.0\]' "$f" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); test -n "$v_line" || { echo "no '## [0.1.0]' heading found"; exit 1; }; test "$u_line" -lt "$v_line" || { echo "ordering violation: [Unreleased] (line $u_line) must stay above [0.1.0] (line $v_line)"; exit 1; }; next_line=$(awk -v start="$u_line" 'NR>start && /^## \[/{print NR; exit}' "$f"); test -n "$next_line" || { echo "could not bound the Unreleased section (no following heading found)"; exit 1; }; section=$(sed -n "${u_line},${next_line}p" "$f"); echo "$section" | grep -Eqi 'validate\.sh|compliance|CI enforcement' && echo PASS || { echo "Unreleased section body lacks phase-002 deliverable phrasing (validate.sh/compliance/CI enforcement)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: CHANGELOG [Unreleased] references the compliance validator and CI workflow: grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' CHANGELOG.md && grep -q 'compliance.yml' CHANGELOG.md -- STRENGTHENED: also asserts the placeholder '_No unreleased changes._' has been removed (replaced with real content), since a file could satisfy the literal grep while leaving the placeholder line present alongside new content.
verification.command: f=CHANGELOG.md; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q '_No unreleased changes_\.' "$f" && { echo "placeholder '_No unreleased changes._' is still present -- must be replaced, not appended alongside"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'tools/validate.sh' "$f" && grep -q 'compliance.yml' "$f" && echo PASS || { echo "missing 'tools/validate.sh' or 'compliance.yml' reference in $f"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: The final tree passes the compliance validator: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0 -- STRENGTHENED per the 014 lesson: asserts the exact summary line 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' in addition to the exit code, so a validator that silently skipped or miscounted a check cannot false-pass this entry.
verification.command: test -f tools/validate.sh || { echo "MISSING tools/validate.sh"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary line 'SUMMARY: 8 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: The release decision is recorded as a human gate (v0.2.0 MINOR) and NOT auto-cut: git tag -l 'v0.2.0' | wc -l outputs 0
verification.command: cnt=$(git tag -l 'v0.2.0' | wc -l); test "$cnt" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "expected 0 v0.2.0 tags (release remains a human gate), found $cnt"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: NIZAM.json remains schema-valid with all indexed paths resolving on the final tree (regression guard via bash tools/validate.sh exit 0) -- STRENGTHENED per the External Anchor Rule (methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md, G6): re-running tools/validate.sh a second time would anchor this check's truth reference on the same artifact test 3 already ran, not an independent source. This command instead validates NIZAM.json directly against registry/nizam-index.schema.json via jsonschema and independently walks every indexed path with a fresh walker script (the same generalised logic proven in contract 012's verification), anchoring on the schema and filesystem themselves rather than on validate.sh's own self-reported summary.

ADDITIONAL (not in feature_list_002.json; a scope-guard per methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md Sec 5, dogfooded on this very contract, matching the pattern used in contract 014): confirms the non-.agent working-tree diff touches exactly CHANGELOG.md, no more and no fewer -- catching an accidental leftover edit or missed file (e.g. any of the already-complete feature 011-014 deliverables, or the [0.1.0] section, all explicitly out of scope per this contract's non_goals).
verification.command: changed=$(git diff --name-only HEAD -- . ':(exclude).agent' | LC_ALL=C sort); expected=$(printf '%s\n' CHANGELOG.md | LC_ALL=C sort); if [ "$changed" = "$expected" ]; then echo PASS; else echo "scope drift -- got:"; echo "$changed"; echo "expected:"; echo "$expected"; exit 1; fi

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/015-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/015-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/015-verify-06.txt for the sixth). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths (e.g. no /tmp scratch-session paths) inside any recorded command. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
